cause black didn't go to a hospital too much, cause they don't have no money or something like that. And no clinic, but now, he, doctor, they were, you know, someone, you know, couldn't even get the people out. But they …

or you didn't do something, it was because of your race, he doesn't see that. You may see partiality. But he doesn't see race. Well, can you think of things that are important, things that you think people should think a…

On the same seat. But the point is, whites have always had a privilege. And do what they want to do. And sit where they want to sit. But you couldn't do it vice versa. That was the essence of it. And I recall one experie…

We didn't, even I didn't come here, you know, sort of from Mars where everything was great and we never had a sense that there was, that could be clashes between white and black. We all, we all knew about those things. I…

Whereas now, you know, the black middle classes out in the suburbs with the white middle class. So in the inner city, you mainly have, you know, kind of these hubs of poverty and, you know, lack of role models, lack of r…
